content.full_text:
State of California

Fair Political Practices Commission

P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

March 14, 1977

A-77-63-004

David M. Schacter
Deputy City Attorney
City of Long Beach
City Hall, Suite 600
Long Beach, CA 90802

Re: Opinion Request
    No. 76-644

Dear Mr. Schacter:

On March 18, 1976, you asked the Fair Political
Practices Commission for a formal opinion concerning whether
the costs of printing and mailing a particular newsletter
from a Long Beach City Councilman were reportable "expendi-
tures" and/or "contributions" within the meaning of the
Political Reform Act. The newsletter in question was printed
and mailed at public expense. In subsequent telephone con-
versations you indicated that you were concerned not only
with the particular newsletter that accompanied your opinion
request but with all newsletters distributed at public ex-
pense by the City of Long Beach.¹

Although the Commission has indicated that certain
types of government expenditures are reportable under the
Political Reform Act as contributions and/or expenditures,
precise guidelines outlining reporting obligations in the
context of publicly financed mass mailings have not yet been
promulgated. However, the staff presently is researching
and drafting regulations that will establish the necessary
guidelines, and these regulations will be responsive to the
question posed in your opinion request. Because the regu-
lations will address the issue posed in your opinion request,
the Commission decided, at its meeting on March 2, 1977,
that a formal opinion would not be necessary and that your

________________
1/ In your opinion request you also asked whether
the enclosed newsletter had a sufficiently legitimate public
purpose to justify printing and mailing it at public expense.
A member of the Commission staff previously has informed you
that this question involves the issue of misuse of public
funds, a question that is beyond the jurisdiction of the
Commission. Letter from Natalie West, November 12, 1976.

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE ADMINISTRATION 1 [illegible]

David M. Schacter
March 14, 1977
Page Two


opinion request should be dismissed. Accordingly, no formal
opinion will be issued in response to your request.

When drafts of the proposed regulations are avail-
able, copies will be provided to you for your comments and
suggestions. Any comments should be directed to Ted Prim,
an attorney on the Commission's staff.

I apologize for the delay in answering your opinion
request. If you have any further questions please feel free
to contact me.

Sincerely,

Michael Bennett
Executive Director

MB:KF:plh
